title: The Canterbury Tales (Harley MS 7334/Furnivall representation)
summary: The repository XML copies U-M/CME encoding AGZ8246.0001.001, an uncorrected OCR and Level-1 structural representation of Furnivall's 1885 one-manuscript Harley MS 7334 edition. Furnivall directly transcribed Harley for the main Canterbury Tales and Gamelyn text, deliberately left an eight-leaf Group F loss unsupplied, retained Tyrwhitt's paragraph divisions for Melibee and the Parson's Tale, and used the Chaucer Society Six-Text only for comparison and navigation. The edition-defined whole separately excerpts a 15-line Nun's Priest end-link from Royal MS 17 D XV, reproduces the bilingual Angelus ad Virginem from Arundel MS 248, and includes W. H. Hooper's 23 Ellesmere-derived and nine Gg.4.27-derived design-bearing plate surfaces. The two complete Internet Archive/Google scans are distinct facsimile carriers whose different title-leaf and repeated-scan states are preserved rather than conflated.
review_notes: Harley order is witness evidence, not a reconstructed authorial final order.
No source-supply edge is asserted for the missing Squire-Franklin material: Furnivall prints the surviving Harley sequence without filling the loss.
The issued woodcut suites are established only for the audited 1885 Harley carrier; no inference is made about earlier Corpus or Lansdowne part carriers.

rights.component: Repository XML, OCR text, and markup as checked in
rights.basis: The CME root Unlicense expressly addresses software and source or binary code but does not clearly license corpus XML, OCR, or markup. Public-domain status of the medieval texts and 1885 edition does not itself settle the separate digital-encoding layer.
notes: Do not extend the software-worded Unlicense to historical XML or markup by implication.

rights.component: U-M digital text and encoding
rights.basis: The current U-M item labels the materials Public Domain and assigns No Copyright-United States.
notes: The XML header preserves an older notice that copyright and other restrictions may apply. The current assertion and historical conflict are both recorded; NoC-US is jurisdiction-specific and is not a license.

rights.basis: The current U-M item applies its Public Domain and No Copyright-United States designation to the offered digital item and linked pages.
notes: The older embedded restriction language remains a conflicting historical notice.

rights.component: Published medieval text, editorial matter, Hooper woodcuts, appendices, and typographical arrangement
rights.basis: The complete carrier was published in 1885, before the current United States pre-1931 public-domain cutoff.

rights.component: Furnivall's and Hooper's published contributions and the edition's typographical arrangement
rights.basis: Furnivall died in 1910 and Hooper in 1912, so life-plus-70 terms in their published contributions have expired; the 1885 typographical arrangement also exceeds the 25-year term. This conclusion is limited to published edition content.

rights.component: Google-scanned PDF carrier of the public-domain 1885 edition

editorial_practices.source_feature: Harley MS 7334 Canterbury Tales sequence, including Gamelyn and a physical loss in Group F
editorial_practices.representation: One-manuscript print in Harley order, without supplying the lost Squire-Franklin material
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall selected Harley as the sole base for the main text because it had already appeared in four Six-Text positions but lacked a simple independent print. Harley's witness-specific order is retained and is not presented here as Chaucer's final authorial order.

editorial_practices.representation: Direct transcription checked against the manuscript twice
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall states that every line was read against Harley twice. Six-Text page references and occasional Ellesmere comparisons are navigational or comparative, not source-supply edges.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Eight lost Harley leaves at the Squire-Franklin transition, about 608 lines within roughly 760 lines missing from the witness overall
editorial_practices.representation: The printed sequence jumps with the witness and does not interpolate another manuscript's text
editorial_practices.description: The edition preserves the material loss as a witness boundary. No conjectural or collateral text is silently inserted into the Harley sequence.
editorial_practices.scope: Group F/VII, Squire's Tale through Franklin's Tale transition

editorial_practices.source_feature: Harley's unlineated and un-paragraphed prose in Melibee and the Parson's Tale
editorial_practices.representation: Paragraph divisions retained from Thomas Tyrwhitt's edition
editorial_practices.description: Furnivall explicitly uses Tyrwhitt's paragraphing for these two prose works because Harley supplies neither paragraph structure nor lineation; this supporting editorial dependency is narrower than textual derivation.
editorial_practices.scope: Tale of Melibee and Parson's Tale

editorial_practices.source_feature: Material explicitly absent from Harley: a Royal end-link, Arundel hymn and facsimile, and Ellesmere/Gg visual exemplars
editorial_practices.representation: Separately labeled textual and visual appendices after the Harley main text
editorial_practices.description: The edition declares that its post-p. 692 material is not in Harley and keeps the Royal, Arundel, Ellesmere, and Gg components visibly distinct from the base-witness transcription.
editorial_practices.scope: Printed pp. 693-696, two unnumbered leaves, and 32 plate surfaces

editorial_practices.source_feature: Complete 778-image carrier with front matter, 696 numbered pages, unnumbered leaves, and 32 plates
editorial_practices.representation: Uncorrected OCR and Level-1 structural encoding with omission containers and PB links
editorial_practices.description: The U-M header states that OCR was not corrected and content was not edited. Running text is encoded while front matter, back matter, and the visual suites are largely represented through omission divisions and page milestones rather than transcribed content.

editorial_practices.source_feature: Bound-carrier sequence, printed pagination, manuscript foliation, and Six-Text navigation
editorial_practices.representation: 778 continuous PB milestones plus print-page, folio, and Six-Text milestones
editorial_practices.description: PB sequence values are unique and continuous from 1 through 778. Print-page milestones, 572 folio occurrences, and 531 unique Six-Text references preserve carrier and editorial navigation anomalies rather than being normalized into a single pagination system.

evidence.citation: General Corpus, CME/source/CME_phase_1-2/agz8246.xml.
evidence.locator: HEADER; TEXT/FRONT, BODY, and BACK; every division, line group, line, paragraph, page break, note, and milestone
evidence.summary: Exact repository source for U-M identifier AGZ8246.0001.001, the 2006 OCR declaration, Furnivall bibliography, encoded Harley sequence, omission boundaries, appendices, page-image references, and milestone systems.
notes: Pinned at CME commit e1d77c88ca561be924b2fedc54dd474be2e7e49c, Git blob 9af44d7d696e001257bb76612a41461db6d080ec.

evidence.citation: Element, hierarchy, count, page-sequence, folio-milestone, Six-Text-milestone, and content-boundary audit of pinned agz8246.xml.
evidence.locator: All DIV1-DIV3, HEAD, LG, L, P, PB, NOTE1, TRAILER, MILESTONE, N, NODE, REF, and SEQ values
evidence.summary: Counts 13 DIV1, 49 DIV2, 41 DIV3, 158 HEAD, 714 LG, 17,674 L, 217 P, 778 PB, 321 NOTE1, 26 TRAILER, and 1,103 MILESTONE elements. Maps ten Harley groups, 49 semantic DIV2 units, a split Retraction, four edition appendices, 24 conventional Chaucerian tales, non-Chaucerian Gamelyn, continuous PB 1-778, and preserved print, folio, and Six-Text anomalies.
notes: Excluding Gamelyn yields 706 LG and 16,772 L; Gamelyn has 8 LG and 902 L. Folio milestones occur 572 times for 570 unique values from 1a to 286a, with duplicated 72a/72b and missing 157a/286b. Six-Text values occur 531 times, uniquely ranging 129-685, and preserve the Group F jump 496-514.

evidence.citation: Kyvero-Press/CME root LICENSE at the pinned submodule commit.
evidence.summary: The license repeatedly scopes itself to software and source or binary code; it does not expressly license historical XML, OCR, editorial data, images, or source editions.

evidence.citation: University of Michigan Library Digital Collections, AGZ8246.0001.001, The Harleian ms. 7334 of Chaucer's Canterbury tales.
evidence.summary: Confirms the current public item, Furnivall title and source edition, public text and page-image routes, and Public Domain/No Copyright-United States statement.

evidence.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Harleian MS. 7334 of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales, ed. Frederick J. Furnivall, Chaucer Society First Series 73 (London: N. Trübner & Co., 1885), complete Harvard/Google carrier.
evidence.locator: Title page; foreword dated 8 January 1886; editorial preface; printed pp. 1-696; two unnumbered leaves; all plate matter
evidence.summary: Complete page-by-page inspection establishes the 1885 imprint, 1886 foreword date, Harley-only main-text policy, twice-checked transcription, preserved Group F loss, Gamelyn's spurious status, Tyrwhitt prose paragraphing, comparative Six-Text navigation, Royal and Arundel textual appendices, and issued Ellesmere/Gg visual suites.
notes: The independent copy2 PDF, SHA-256 814aec2a02c9ab3e688cc3b4940020b25459bd07adf23234f35f0d784cb301a0, corroborates the complete edition while preserving distinct scan-carrier states.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for harleianmschauc01chaugoog.
evidence.summary: Identifies the 799-page Harvard-contributed Google carrier, Google Books ID, direct derivatives, bibliographic metadata, and exact file inventory.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive OCR and scandata derivatives for harleianmschauc01chaugoog.
evidence.summary: Complete machine OCR and leaf-level scandata preserve searchable text and the carrier's page-object sequence separately from the U-M encoding.
notes: Scandata is source-cache/agz8246/furnivall-harleian-ms-7334-1885-harvard-google-scandata.xml, SHA-256 7f69ed3da1016dd89b6d9d937bd640f2e780195b3be2487ea646e53f39172f8c.

evidence.citation: Page-by-page audit of Internet Archive/Google item harleianmschauc00chaugoog.
evidence.locator: All 800 PDF pages, including title, main text, appendices, and visual suites
evidence.summary: Verifies an independent complete carrier of the 1885 Harley edition. Provider metadata does not name its source library; a visible Stanford Library mark supports only a cautious carrier observation, not a resolved holding claim.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive metadata for harleianmschauc00chaugoog.
evidence.summary: Identifies an 800-page Google carrier and exact derivatives while leaving the source-library field unknown.

evidence.citation: Internet Archive OCR and scandata derivatives for harleianmschauc00chaugoog.
evidence.summary: Complete machine OCR and leaf-level scandata preserve searchable text and page-object structure for the independent carrier.
notes: Scandata is source-cache/agz8246/furnivall-harleian-ms-7334-1885-google-copy2-scandata.xml, SHA-256 6dfcaaac8939e86638db63aa1dde37bc80d4245a521c51a7553a60d2331145d6.

evidence.citation: Google Books usage notice embedded at the beginning of both cached Google-derived edition PDFs.
evidence.locator: Opening notice page; corroborated in the independent copy2 PDF
evidence.summary: Google identifies the underlying book as public domain in the United States while requesting attribution and limiting file use to personal non-commercial purposes. The notice is treated as a service-file term, not as copyright in the historical edition.

evidence.citation: Cross-carrier audit of all Ellesmere- and Gg-derived design-bearing surfaces in both complete 1885 Harley scans.
evidence.locator: Harvard PDF 719-793; copy2 PDF 721-787; corresponding Huntington and CUDL source loci
evidence.summary: The Harvard carrier has the Ellesmere title at PDF 719 and 23 cuts at 721-765 odd; its Gg title is 767 and its nine distinct surfaces are 769, 771, 773, 775, 777, 787, 789, 791, and 793, while 779/781/783 repeat Pardoner and 785 repeats Monk. Copy2 has the Ellesmere title at 721 and 23 cuts at 723-767 odd; nine Gg surfaces at 769-785 odd; no separate Gg title leaf; and a Canon's Yeoman repeat at 787. Both therefore contain 32 distinct design-bearing surfaces, but their scan states differ.
notes: The 23 Ellesmere loci run from f.10r/c26 through f.206r/c418. The Gg loci are f.186r/c383, f.192v/c396, f.222r/c455, f.306r/c623, f.352r/c715, f.395r/c801, f.416r/c843, f.432r/c879, and f.433r/c881. This proves issuance only in the audited 1885 Harley carrier; it does not establish plate presence in earlier Corpus or Lansdowne carriers.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Harley MS 7334, record 040-002053188.
evidence.summary: Confirms shelfmark, c. 1405-1415 production, 286-folio parchment extent, script, likely English production region, Canterbury Tales content, physical consultation route, and current unavailability of linked images.

evidence.citation: Norman Blake Canterbury Tales Project, Ha4 multitext edition and complete downloadable dataset.
evidence.locator: Ha4 subset: 54 files; TG-Ha4-Est-3.txt contains Gamelyn
evidence.summary: Provides an independently downloadable diplomatic transcription of Harley MS 7334 within an eight-manuscript, 748-file dataset, with a CC BY-NC 4.0 project license.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Royal MS 17 D XV, record 040-002107393.
evidence.locator: Royal MS 17 D XV; Furnivall target f. 284r
evidence.summary: Confirms the paper manuscript, second-half-fifteenth-century date, current shelfmark, physical-consultation and reproduction routes, and lack of a public complete image set for the exact appendix locus.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue, Arundel MS 248, record 040-002039531.
evidence.summary: Confirms the manuscript identity and current custody and supports physical-consultation and reproduction-request routes; no public complete image set for the exact Angelus ad Virginem locus was found.

evidence.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Canterbury Tales of Chaucer, ed. Thomas Tyrwhitt, vol. 2 (London, 1775), Internet Archive bib_fict_4103376_2.
evidence.locator: Complete 342-page volume; Tale of Melibee paragraph structure
evidence.summary: The complete scan identifies Tyrwhitt's edition and permits direct comparison of Melibee paragraph divisions cited by Furnivall; OCR, metadata, and scandata derivatives are cached separately.
notes: OCR SHA-256 bc07902398179293d7fbb3a2450cb2dfbc1d86c6afbccec59fcd2d64ede6d2fc; metadata 639202bcec47167dcb38673872b38588f52720aaa8abcb8bfdb628fde909aaa8; scandata dea9fa36278aaef6055f87eba934df8cae727ee0f46aa1e24ac93d886aaf6d0c.

evidence.citation: Geoffrey Chaucer, The Canterbury Tales of Chaucer, ed. Thomas Tyrwhitt, vol. 3 (London, 1775), Internet Archive bib_fict_4103376_3.
evidence.locator: Complete 342-page volume; Parson's Tale paragraph structure
evidence.summary: The complete scan identifies Tyrwhitt's edition and permits direct comparison of Parson's Tale paragraph divisions cited by Furnivall; OCR, metadata, and scandata derivatives are cached separately.
notes: OCR SHA-256 13deadd032c9074dbc3cca494961b4958084cfbf191e2370dc0af4822e826554; metadata 6ac9e0a3af2e2b06f1b6d6aa27886808ba779bb9007fc8acd8633dd9120add5b; scandata 44e981137e463ab634e83df61edf840331ea71a30a2f73238955fa529300c8ae.

evidence.citation: A Six-Text Print of Chaucer's Canterbury Tales in Parallel Columns, ed. Frederick J. Furnivall, Parts I-II, Internet Archive partial-series scan.
evidence.summary: Provides a supporting partial surrogate for the comparison series. It contains no copy of the 1885 Harley target edition and is not treated as a direct textual ancestor; qpdf reports malformed indirect references and hint-table warnings, but the preserved provider bytes remain readable.

evidence.citation: The Huntington Digital Library, mssEL 26 C 9, The Ellesmere Chaucer, and its IIIF Presentation 2 manifest.
evidence.summary: Official record and 480-canvas manifest for the Ellesmere witness, its c. 1400-1410 date, 23 relevant teller portraits, image services, and provider rights-policy link.

evidence.citation: Deep inventory, ZIP-integrity, and target-locus audit of the complete local Huntington Ellesmere 1600-pixel IIIF bundle.
evidence.locator: All 480 canvases; 23 target folios from f.10r/c26 through f.206r/c418
evidence.summary: Verifies complete provider-published coverage, ZIP CRC integrity, the exact inventory, and all twenty-three visual-source portrait loci without cropping the manuscript object.

evidence.citation: The Huntington, Library Rights and Permissions.
evidence.summary: States that Huntington does not require permission or charge fees for public-domain material or material whose copyright it does not own, requests source citation, and leaves copyright determination and any needed permissions to the researcher.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library, MS Gg.4.27(1), object description and downloadable TEI metadata.
evidence.summary: Confirms the manuscript identity, shelfmark, Canterbury Tales content, physical and bibliographic description, and visual-source loci; the complete catalog TEI is cached with fixity.
notes: The TEI parses, but its provider bytes repeat xml:id values Parkes1979, Caldwell1943, and Seymour1997; no local normalization was applied.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library, IIIF Presentation manifest for MS Gg.4.27(1).
evidence.summary: Official complete presentation inventory supporting the nine design-bearing source pages and separate CUDL licenses for downloadable images and metadata.

evidence.citation: Deep inventory, image, ZIP-integrity, and target-locus audit of the complete local CUDL MS Gg.4.27(1) 1600-pixel IIIF bundle.
evidence.locator: All 1,144 canvases; nine discontiguous target canvases 383-881
evidence.summary: Verifies complete provider coverage and ZIP CRC integrity and locates all twelve figures: six tellers plus paired allegories at f.416r/c843, f.432r/c879, and f.433r/c881. Canvases 875 and 877 are blank and textual states, not the last two allegory surfaces.

evidence.citation: Cambridge Digital Library, attribution and licensing statement embedded in the MS Gg.4.27(1) IIIF presentation.
evidence.summary: Assigns downloadable images Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial 4.0 and metadata Creative Commons Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0, separately from underlying manuscript status.

evidence.citation: Deutsche Nationalbibliothek, GND 117540579, Furnivall, Frederick James.
evidence.locator: Preferred name; birth 1825-02-04; death 1910-07-02; CC0 metadata license
evidence.summary: Official authority evidence for the editor's identity and life dates and for the record's CC0 metadata assertion.

evidence.citation: British Museum Collections Online, William Harcourt Hooper, BIOG32010.
evidence.summary: Identifies William Harcourt Hooper as a British wood engraver and printmaker who lived 1834-1912, corroborating the named W. H. Hooper who copied and cut the edition's woodcuts.

evidence.citation: British Library Archives and Manuscripts Catalogue licensing statement.
evidence.summary: States that catalog text is available under Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 except where otherwise stated; this catalog-data license is separate from manuscript, physical-property, and reproduction layers.

evidence.citation: United States Copyright Office, Circular 15A, Duration of Copyright, revised April 2026.
evidence.summary: Supports the 2026 rule that works published in the United States before 1 January 1931 are in the public domain.

evidence.citation: United States Code, title 17, section 105, Subject matter of copyright: United States Government works.
evidence.summary: Official statutory text stating that United States copyright protection is unavailable for United States Government works, subject to the section's specified exceptions.

evidence.citation: UK Intellectual Property Office, Copyright Notice: Duration of Copyright (term).
evidence.summary: Official guidance states life-plus-70 terms for literary and artistic works, 25 years for typographical arrangements, and a possible term through 31 December 2039 for qualifying literary, dramatic, musical, and engraving works unpublished at the end of 1988.

open_questions.question: Should this carrier be cited strictly as 1885, as 1886, or as an 1885 imprint with an 1886-dated foreword?
next_steps: Retain the title-page imprint and dated foreword separately.
Consult Chaucer Society issue records or a copy-specific accession record before collapsing them into one publication date.

open_questions.question: Which institutional copy was digitized as harleianmschauc00chaugoog?
next_steps: Ask Internet Archive or Google Books for source-copy metadata tied to Google Books ID PEHGaZd-ka0C.
Treat the visible Stanford Library mark as suggestive carrier evidence, not a resolved provenance statement.

open_questions.question: Does U-M's current No Copyright-United States statement supersede the older restriction language embedded in the 2006 XML header?
next_steps: Request written clarification from U-M Digital Collections.
Until clarified, preserve both statements and do not interpret NoC-US as a redistribution license.

open_questions.question: Can complete public facsimiles of Harley MS 7334, Royal MS 17 D XV f. 284r, or Arundel MS 248 f. 154r be obtained from a current institutional service?
next_steps: Monitor the British Library catalog and digitized-manuscripts replacement services after service restoration.
If direct image evidence is required, request the exact folios through the British Library reproduction route subject to condition and fees.
Keep the complete Norman Blake Harley transcription as a supporting encoding, not a substitute facsimile.
